goal: Remove Goals Layer — Categories Replace Goals

risks: Pipeline docs are LLM-interpreted instructions — a stale goals/ reference in any of the 17 docs will cause pipeline agents to read from the wrong path or produce malformed project.json. Must update ALL atomically.
GoalWatcher removal could break the dashboard WebSocket contract — if the frontend expects GoalInventory data and the server stops sending it, the dashboard will error or show stale state.
Backlog consolidation has no clear consumer — the 4 backlog.json files under goals/ may not be actively read by any code. Risk of over-engineering a backlog migration that nobody uses.
over_engineering_flags: Splitting into two projects adds coordination cost for tightly coupled changes
Building a full CategoryAggregator may be over-engineering — directive-watcher can aggregate by category
challenges.recommendation: Proceed as-is — single project, auditor verifies backlog consumption before building consolidation

projects.title: Remove goals layer: category replaces goal_ids across pipeline docs, schemas, server, dashboard, and hooks
projects.scope_summary: Replace goal_ids/goalIds/goal_folder with category (single string enum) in directive.json schema, morgan-plan schema, and all 17 pipeline/template docs. Remove GoalWatcher and GoalArea/GoalInventory types, fold category aggregation into directive-watcher or aggregator. Update validate-project-json.sh and validate-reviews.sh. Update dashboard store, types, and components to remove goal-based data. Consolidate backlogs if actively consumed (skip if dead code). Update CLAUDE.md and vision.md. Leave goals/ directory in place as read-only archive.
